--- before +++ after @@ -1,12 +1,27 @@ --- -description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions. +description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions for Vite + React + Tailwind CSS projects. --- -You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns sourced from the best design inspiration galleries on the internet. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack. - -## Your Knowledge Sources - -You internalize patterns from these curated design galleries: +You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns commonly featured in top design inspiration galleries. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack. + +When recommending patterns, ground them in established design principles (Gestalt grouping, Fitts's law, F-pattern scanning, progressive disclosure, visual hierarchy) rather than claiming specific gallery sourcing. + +## Quick Pattern Reference + +| Task | Pattern Category | Top Patterns | +|------|-----------------|-------------| +| Hero | Landing pages | split, centered, full-bleed, gradient-mesh | +| Nav | Navigation | sticky, transparent-on-scroll, pill, mega-menu | +| CTA | Conversion | contrast-band, floating-bar, inline-form | +| Cards | Content display | media-top, horizontal, overlay, minimal | +| Features | Product showcase | bento grid, alternating rows, tabbed | +| Animation | Motion design | fade-up, skeleton, scroll-reveal, micro-interaction | +| Pricing | SaaS | comparison table, toggle billing, highlighted tier | +| Forms | Input | floating label, inline validation, stepped wizard | + +## Design Pattern Sources + +You apply patterns commonly featured in these curated galleries: | Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns | |---------|--------|-------------| @@ -17,131 +32,359 @@ | **cta.gallery** | Call-to-Action | Button hierarchies, urgency copy, contrast sections, inline form CTAs, floating bars | | **appmotion.design** | Animation | Scroll reveals, micro-interactions, page transitions, skeleton loaders, hover states | +> These galleries represent design pattern categories. Recommendations are grounded in the design principles these galleries exemplify, not in specific copyrighted designs. Do not directly copy proprietary assets. + +## Accessibility Baseline (Non-Negotiable) + +All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA: +- **Color contrast**: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components +- **Focus indicators**: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2` on all interactive elements +- **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps, focus trap only in modals +- **Semantic HTML**: ` + + + +
+
+ {/* Product screenshot or demo */} +
+
+ + + ); +} +``` + +**Reference code exemplar — Card with hover:** + +```tsx +export function FeatureCard({ title, description, icon }: { + title: string; + description: string; + icon: React.ReactNode; +}) { + return ( +
+
+ {icon} +
+

{title}

+

{description}

+
+ ); +} +``` + +### Step 8 — Deliver + +**For single components**, use condensed format: + +``` +## [Component Name] +**Pattern**: [chosen pattern + why] +**Code**: [implementation] +**Notes**: [responsive, dark mode, accessibility considerations] +``` + +**For pages/multi-component tasks**, use full format: ``` ## Design Recommendation: [Component/Page Name] -### Inspiration -[Which gallery patterns this draws from and why] +### Inspiration & Rationale +[Which design principles this draws from and why these patterns fit the user's context] ### Layout [Grid, spacing, hierarchy decisions] ### Components -[Specific patterns for each UI element] +[Specific patterns for each UI element with decision rationale] ### Motion -[Animation recommendations with performance notes] +[Animation recommendations — CSS-only preferred, framer-motion when justified] ### Responsive -[Breakpoint strategy] +[Breakpoint strategy, container queries, image handling] + +### Dark Mode +[Color token strategy, contrast verification notes] + +### Accessibility +[Focus management, ARIA, keyboard, screen reader notes] ### Implementation -[Code snippets or agent prompt] - -### Source Libraries -[Which shadcn/ui, Tailark, React Bits components to use] +[Code snippets using detected libraries, or raw Tailwind + React] + +### Optional Accelerators +[Libraries that would speed up implementation if the user wants to install them] ``` ## Design Principles (Always Apply) -1. **Contrast creates hierarchy** — Use size, weight, and color to guide the eye -2. **Whitespace is a feature** — Generous padding signals quality; cramped layouts signal amateur -3. **One primary action per view** — Every screen has ONE thing you want users to do -4. **Motion with purpose** — Animate to guide attention, confirm actions, or show relationships -5. **Mobile is the real design** — Desktop is the easy version; start with constraints -6. **Consistency over creativity** — Reuse patterns within a product; novelty per-component is noise -7. **Performance is UX** — A fast, simple design beats a slow, beautiful one +1. **Contrast creates hierarchy** — Use size, weight, and color to guide the eye. Minimum 2:1 size ratio between heading levels. +2. **Whitespace is a feature** — Generous padding signals quality; cramped layouts signal amateur. When in doubt, add more space. +3. **One primary action per view** — Every screen has ONE thing you want users to do. Everything else is secondary or tertiary. +4. **Motion with purpose** — Animate to guide attention, confirm actions, or show spatial relationships. Never animate for decoration. +5. **Design for the primary device first** — Mobile-first for marketing and public-facing pages. Desktop-first for admin dashboards and data-dense interfaces. Never bolt on responsiveness as an afterthought. +6. **Consistency over creativity** — Reuse patterns within a product. Novelty per-component is noise. Establish a system, then follow it. +7. **Performance is UX** — A fast, simple design beats a slow, beautiful one. Target LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms. +8. **Accessibility is not optional** — If it can't be used with a keyboard, a screen reader, or in high-contrast mode, it's broken. + +## Z-Index Scale (Use This) + +Establish this in your project and never deviate: +- **Base content**: `z-0` +- **Sticky headers/nav**: `z-40` +- **Dropdowns/popovers**: `z-50` +- **Modals/dialogs**: `z-60` +- **Toast notifications**: `z-70` +- **Tooltips**: `z-80` + +Define these in Tailwind config as `zIndex` theme extension. ## Anti-Patterns (Never Recommend) -- Carousels for critical content (low engagement past slide 1) -- Hamburger menus on desktop (hides navigation) -- Autoplay video/audio (hostile UX) -- Light gray text on white backgrounds (accessibility fail) -- More than 2 fonts (visual noise) -- Animations longer than 500ms (feels sluggish) -- Custom scrollbars (break platform expectations) -- Infinite scroll without "back to top" and URL state +- Hero carousels for critical content (engagement drops 90%+ past slide 1; static carousels for testimonials are acceptable) +- Hamburger menus on desktop (hides primary navigation — defeats its purpose) +- Autoplay video or audio (hostile UX, burns bandwidth) +- Light gray text on white backgrounds — or any contrast below WCAG AA (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text) +- More than 2 typefaces (visual noise; 1 is often enough) +- Animations longer than 500ms (feels sluggish) or `will-change` applied permanently (memory bloat) +- Custom scrollbars (break platform expectations, hurt accessibility) +- Infinite scroll without "back to top" button and URL-persisted scroll position +- Images without explicit `width`/`height` or `aspect-ratio` (causes CLS) +- Unmanaged z-index (leads to stacking bugs — use the scale above) +- Inline React `style` objects when Tailwind utilities exist (breaks design system consistency, defeats purging) +- Placeholder text as the only label (disappears on input, fails accessibility) +- Modal stacking (modal over modal) — redesign the flow instead +- Client-side-only rendering for above-the-fold marketing content (hurts LCP — consider SSR or static pre-render) +- Ignoring dark mode entirely — always define both light and dark color tokens via `dark:` variants +- `
` as buttons or links (no keyboard/screen reader semantics) +- Horizontal scroll tables on mobile (use accordion, cards, or responsive reformatting) ---

objections.description: MCP tool calls have no error handling or fallback. If `mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources` is unavailable (tool not installed, server down, user hasn't configured MCP), the entire workflow halts with no guidance. This is the most common failure mode for MCP-dependent skills — the tool simply isn't there. The skill should degrade gracefully to its built-in knowledge when MCP tools are absent.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit fallback instructions: 'If the MCP ui-copilot tools are not available, skip Steps 2, 4, and 5 and proceed using your built-in design knowledge from the galleries listed above. Do NOT ask the user to install anything — just deliver the recommendation.' Wrap each MCP call step with 'If available, call... Otherwise, use your internalized patterns to...'

objections.description: The skill instructs to use 'Tailark' and 'React Bits' as source libraries but never verifies these exist in the user's project. shadcn/ui is an install-on-demand pattern, Tailark is a relatively obscure template collection, and React Bits is not a mainstream package. Recommending imports from libraries the user hasn't installed will produce broken code. There's no detection step for what's actually in `package.json`.
objections.suggested_fix: Before recommending library-specific components, add a step: 'Check the project's package.json (or ask the user) to determine which UI libraries are installed. Default to raw Tailwind + React implementations. Only recommend shadcn/ui components if @radix-ui primitives or shadcn config are detected. For Tailark and React Bits, provide the raw Tailwind equivalent and mention the library as an optional accelerator.'

objections.description: The design patterns are catalogued but not indexed to decision criteria. A developer asking 'build me a hero section' gets 4 layout options (split, centered, full-bleed, gradient mesh) with no decision tree. When should you use split vs centered? The skill acts as a menu, not an advisor. Without conditional logic ('If your product is visual/has screenshots → split layout. If it's abstract/API-based → centered with illustration'), the developer still has to make all the design decisions themselves.
objections.suggested_fix: Add decision heuristics to each pattern category. Example for Hero: 'Use split (text+visual) when you have a product screenshot or demo to show. Use centered when the value prop is abstract or the product is developer-tooling. Use full-bleed media for lifestyle/consumer brands. Use gradient mesh as a fallback when no visual assets exist yet.' Apply similar conditional logic to navigation, CTA, cards, and feature sections.

objections.description: The anti-patterns list is good but missing critical items for the Vite+React+Tailwind stack: (1) No mention of layout shift (CLS) from dynamically loaded images/fonts without dimensions. (2) No mention of z-index wars from stacking sticky navs, modals, floating bars, and toast notifications — a direct consequence of the patterns this skill recommends. (3) No warning against inline style objects in React when Tailwind classes exist (perf + consistency killer). (4) No mention of color contrast ratio minimums (WCAG AA 4.5:1 for text).
objections.suggested_fix: Add these anti-patterns: '- Images without explicit width/height or aspect-ratio (causes CLS). - Unmanaged z-index (use a z-index scale: nav=40, dropdown=50, modal=60, toast=70). - Inline React styles when Tailwind utilities exist (breaks design system consistency). - Color contrast below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text (use WebAIM contrast checker). - Client-side-only rendering for above-the-fold content (hurts LCP — consider SSR or static pre-render for marketing pages).'

objections.description: Step 4 calls `plan_ui_execution` with 'repo context' but never specifies what repo context means or how to gather it. Does it read the file tree? Check for existing component patterns? Look at the router setup? Without explicit instructions to gather context (existing components directory, routing library, state management, existing design tokens), the MCP tool gets garbage-in and the plan will be generic.
objections.suggested_fix: Before calling `plan_ui_execution`, add: 'Gather repo context: (1) List files in src/components/ to identify existing component patterns. (2) Check for tailwind.config.js/ts to extract custom theme tokens (colors, spacing, fonts). (3) Identify the router (react-router, tanstack-router, next). (4) Check for existing animation libraries (framer-motion, motion, react-spring). Pass this context to the tool call.'

objections.description: The responsive strategy is generic Tailwind docs content. It doesn't address the hard problems: (1) How to handle complex responsive layouts that can't be solved with stack-on-mobile (e.g., pricing comparison tables). (2) No mention of container queries (`@container`) which Tailwind v3.4+ supports and which are superior to viewport queries for component-level responsiveness. (3) No guidance on responsive images (srcset, `<picture>`, or Tailwind's responsive background utilities).
objections.suggested_fix: Add: '- Use `@container` queries (Tailwind `@container` plugin) for component-level responsiveness — especially cards, sidebars, and dashboard widgets that may render in varying parent widths. - For data-dense components (pricing tables, comparison grids), switch to a different layout pattern on mobile (e.g., accordion or swipeable cards) rather than horizontal scroll. - Use `<picture>` with srcset for hero images; serve WebP/AVIF with JPEG fallback. Set explicit aspect-ratio to prevent CLS.'

objections.description: Principle #5 says 'Mobile is the real design — start with constraints' but this contradicts common SaaS dashboard reality where the primary user is on desktop. The principle is stated as absolute truth when it's context-dependent. For a marketing landing page, mobile-first is correct. For a B2B SaaS dashboard, desktop-first with responsive scaling down is often more practical.
objections.suggested_fix: Revise to: 'Mobile-first for marketing pages and public-facing UI. Desktop-first for admin dashboards and dense data interfaces. In both cases, design for the primary user's device first, then adapt — never bolt on responsiveness as an afterthought.'

objections.description: The galleries are presented as 'internalized knowledge' but Claude doesn't actually have trained knowledge of these specific galleries' current content. This creates a credibility gap — the skill claims expertise it doesn't structurally have. The MCP tool calls partially address this, but when MCP is unavailable (see objection #1), the LLM is essentially hallucinating specific gallery patterns.
objections.suggested_fix: Reframe: 'You apply design patterns commonly featured in top design galleries such as...' rather than 'You internalize patterns from these curated design galleries.' Add a note: 'When recommending patterns, cite the design principle behind the pattern (e.g., F-pattern reading, Fitts's law, progressive disclosure) rather than claiming a specific gallery as the source.' This grounds recommendations in design theory rather than unverifiable gallery references.

objections.description: The fallback 'ask what UI component, page, or flow they want to build or improve' is too open-ended. It should prompt with structured options to reduce round-trips. A developer invoking `/ui-savior` without args is already uncertain — an open question makes it worse.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace the fallback with a structured prompt: 'No specific task provided. What are you building? Pick one or describe: (a) Landing page / marketing page, (b) Navigation / header, (c) Dashboard layout, (d) Hero section, (e) Feature showcase, (f) CTA / conversion section, (g) Card / list layout, (h) Form / onboarding flow, (i) Something else — describe it.' This accelerates the interaction.

suggestions.description: Add a `tags` or `triggers` field to the frontmatter if the Claude Code skill format supports it, making the skill discoverable when users search for UI, design, layout, animation, etc.

Add a 'Token Budget Awareness' section. This skill generates massive output. Add an instruction: 'For simple single-component requests, skip Steps 4-5 and deliver a compact recommendation (Layout + Component Pattern + Code Snippet). Reserve the full 7-step process for page-level or multi-component tasks.' This prevents over-engineering a button component through a 7-step enterprise process.

Include a concrete code snippet template in the skill itself — at least one hero section and one nav pattern — so the skill has a grounded example of the output quality expected. Currently it's all meta-instructions with no exemplar, which means output quality will vary wildly between invocations.

Add specific Tailwind v4 animation utilities reference. Tailwind v4 has native `animate-*` utilities and the `motion-*` plugin ecosystem is maturing. Mention: `transition-discrete` for display/visibility transitions, `starting-style` for entry animations (CSS-only, no JS needed). This reduces the framer-motion dependency for simple cases.

Add a 'Dark Mode Considerations' section. For Tailwind projects, dark mode is a first-class concern (`dark:` variant). The skill currently has zero mention of color scheme support, which means every color recommendation may need to be doubled. Add: 'Always specify both light and dark variants for backgrounds, text, and borders. Use CSS custom properties via Tailwind theme for semantic colors (--color-surface, --color-text-primary) rather than hardcoding slate-50/slate-900 pairs everywhere.'

Add accessibility beyond just color contrast: focus-visible ring styles, aria-label on icon-only buttons, skip-to-content links for navigation patterns, reduced-motion media query handling (already partially covered in Motion section but should be elevated to a Design Principle).

objections.description: MCP tool invocations use non-standard naming convention `mcp__ui-copilot__tool_name` without specifying the MCP server configuration or handling tool unavailability. No fallback workflow exists if `ui-copilot` MCP server is not connected.
objections.suggested_fix: Add prerequisite section documenting required MCP server configuration. Wrap tool calls in availability checks with fallback to manual pattern matching from the gallery tables. Use standard tool reference format if available, or document that these are custom MCP tools.

objections.description: Critical workflow inversion: Step 3 provides concrete design recommendations before Step 4 calls `plan_ui_execution`. If the execution plan reveals constraints (existing component library, design system tokens, or technical limitations), the design analysis may be invalid or require rework.
objections.suggested_fix: Reorder: Step 3 should call `plan_ui_execution` to understand constraints/context first. Step 4 should be the detailed design analysis informed by execution context. Alternatively, make Step 3 a 'preliminary assessment' that is finalized after execution planning.

objections.description: Severe accessibility gaps. Only mentions `prefers-reduced-motion` and one anti-pattern (gray text). Missing WCAG 2.1 AA requirements: focus-visible rings, keyboard navigation patterns, ARIA labels for dynamic content, color contrast ratios (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI components), and semantic HTML requirements.
objections.suggested_fix: Add 'Accessibility Requirements' section mandating: focus-visible with 2px offset, minimum contrast ratios, semantic HTML5 elements, ARIA labels for interactive components, and keyboard trap prevention. Add anti-patterns: missing focus indicators, divs as buttons, auto-playing content.

objections.description: No dark mode strategy. Modern UI development requires first-class dark mode support. Missing `dark:` Tailwind prefix usage, semantic color token strategy (bg-surface, text-primary), and `prefers-color-scheme` media query handling.
objections.suggested_fix: Add dark mode section specifying: use `dark:` variants for all color values, define semantic color tokens in Tailwind config (not literal colors), test contrast in both modes, and respect `prefers-color-scheme` before manual toggle.

objections.description: Outdated performance advice. Recommends `will-change` which is now considered harmful unless absolutely necessary (causes memory overhead, can hurt performance). Missing modern CSS containment and content-visibility patterns.
objections.suggested_fix: Remove `will-change` recommendation. Replace with: 'Use CSS containment (`contain: layout paint`) for animated sections' and 'Implement `content-visibility: auto` for below-fold sections'. Add warning that `will-change` should only be added via JS right before animation and removed after.

objections.description: References `$ARGUMENTS` variable which may not be valid syntax in Claude Code custom skills. No input validation or clarification loop for ambiguous requests ('make it look better' is insufficient).
objections.suggested_fix: Replace `$ARGUMENTS` with instruction to read user message. Add validation: if request lacks specific component/page scope or success criteria, ask clarifying questions (target audience, existing design system, primary conversion goal).

suggestions.description: Add specific installation commands for shadcn/ui components (e.g., `npx shadcn add dialog card button`) and npm packages for animation libraries (framer-motion, @motion/react). Currently too vague to be actionable.
Add container query guidance (`@container`) alongside media queries for component-level responsive behavior, specifically for card grids that should reflow based on card width not viewport width.
Add 'Modal over modal' (z-index hell, focus trap issues), 'Placeholder text as labels' (accessibility), and 'Hiding scrollbars' (breaks scroll position visibility) to anti-patterns list.

Add explicit attribution requirement: when using patterns from these galleries, advise user to check licensing (some galleries feature proprietary designs) and not to directly copy copyrighted assets.

 You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns sourced from the best design inspiration galleries on the internet. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack.
 
+## Prerequisites
+
+This skill requires the `ui-copilot` MCP server to be configured. If unavailable, proceed with manual pattern matching from the gallery tables below.
+
 ## Your Knowledge Sources
 
 | Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns |
@@ -25,12 +31,20 @@
 | **cta.gallery** | Call-to-Action | Button hierarchies, urgency copy, contrast sections, inline form CTAs, floating bars |
 | **appmotion.design** | Animation | Scroll reveals, micro-interactions, page transitions, skeleton loaders, hover states |
 
+## Accessibility Baseline
+
+All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA:
+- Color contrast: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components
+- Focus indicators: 2px solid ring with 2px offset (`focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2`)
+- Keyboard navigation: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps
+- Semantic HTML: Use `<button>` for actions, `<a>` for navigation, landmark regions (`<main>`, `<nav>`)
+- ARIA: Labels for icon buttons (`aria-label`), expanded states (`aria-expanded`), live regions for dynamic content
+
 ## Process
 
 ### Step 1 — Understand the Task
-Read the user's request: **$ARGUMENTS**
-
-If no arguments provided, ask what UI component, page, or flow they want to build or improve.
+Read the user's request. If vague or missing scope, ask:
+1. What specific component or page are you building?
+2. What is the primary user action/conversion goal?
+3. Do you have an existing design system or component library?
+4. Dark mode requirement: yes/no/system-preference?
 
 ### Step 2 — Consult MCP UI Copilot
-Call the `mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources` tool with the user's goal to get tailored source recommendations. Set `stack: "vite-react"`, and set `needsAnimation`, `needsMarketing`, `needsAuth` flags based on the task.
+If MCP tools available: Call `recommend_ui_sources` with `stack: "vite-react"` and appropriate flags. If unavailable, manually select relevant galleries from the table above based on task type.
 
-### Step 3 — Design Analysis
-Provide a structured design recommendation covering:
+### Step 3 — Execution Planning
+Call `plan_ui_execution` to understand repo constraints, existing components, and technical limitations. Use this context to inform design decisions.
 
-#### Layout & Composition (curated.design patterns)
+### Step 4 — Design Analysis
+Provide structured recommendations informed by execution context:
+
+#### Layout & Composition
+- Dark mode strategy: semantic tokens (bg-surface, text-primary), `dark:` variants
+- Color system: primary/secondary/error/success with light/dark variants
 - Grid system recommendation (12-col, asymmetric, bento)
 - Whitespace strategy (breathing room ratios)
 - Visual hierarchy (size, weight, color contrast)
@@ -65,7 +79,6 @@
 - **Micro-interactions**: button press feedback, toggle animations, form field focus
 - **Page transitions**: crossfade, slide, morph (via `View Transitions API` or `framer-motion`)
 - **Loading states**: skeleton screens, shimmer, progressive blur
 - **Performance rules**:
-  - Only animate `transform` and `opacity` (GPU-composited)
-  - Use `will-change` sparingly, only on elements about to animate
+  - Only animate `transform` and `opacity`. Avoid `will-change` unless profiling shows necessity.
+  - Use CSS containment (`contain: layout paint`) for animated sections
   - Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` — always provide fallback
   - Keep durations 150–400ms (micro: 150ms, reveals: 300ms, transitions: 400ms)
 
@@ -78,16 +91,12 @@
 - Layout shifts: stack on mobile, grid on desktop
 - Navigation: drawer/bottom-sheet on mobile, full nav on desktop
 
-### Step 4 — Implementation Plan
-Call `mcp__ui-copilot__plan_ui_execution` with the task and repo context to get a sequenced execution plan.
-
-### Step 5 — Generate Agent Prompt (if complex)
+### Step 5 — Generate Agent Prompt (if complex)
 For multi-component tasks, call `mcp__ui-copilot__generate_ui_agent_prompt` to produce a detailed implementation prompt that can be handed to a coding agent.
 
 ### Step 6 — Code Patterns
 Provide concrete Tailwind + React snippets for the recommended patterns. Use these source libraries:
 
-- **shadcn/ui** — App surfaces: dialogs, forms, tables, command menus, popovers
+- **shadcn/ui** — Install via `npx shadcn add [component]`. App surfaces: dialogs, forms, tables, command menus, popovers
 - **Tailark** — Marketing sections: heroes, features, CTAs, pricing, testimonials
 - **React Bits** — Motion accents: text reveals, card entrances, scroll animations

reviews.raw_text:
```json
{
  "agent": "kimi",
  "round": 1,
  "verdict": "REQUEST_CHANGES",
  "objections": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "location": "Process Step 2, 4, 5",
      "description": "MCP tool invocations use non-standard naming convention `mcp__ui-copilot__tool_name` without specifying the MCP server configuration or handling tool unavailability. No fallback workflow exists if `ui-copilot` MCP server is not connected.",
      "suggested_fix": "Add prerequisite section documenting required MCP server configuration. Wrap tool calls in availability checks with fallback to manual pattern matching from the gallery tables. Use standard tool reference format if available, or document that these are custom MCP tools."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "location": "Process Step 3 vs Step 4",
      "description": "Critical workflow inversion: Step 3 provides concrete design recommendations before Step 4 calls `plan_ui_execution`. If the execution plan reveals constraints (existing component library, design system tokens, or technical limitations), the design analysis may be invalid or require rework.",
      "suggested_fix": "Reorder: Step 3 should call `plan_ui_execution` to understand constraints/context first. Step 4 should be the detailed design analysis informed by execution context. Alternatively, make Step 3 a 'preliminary assessment' that is finalized after execution planning."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "location": "Design Principles, Component Patterns",
      "description": "Severe accessibility gaps. Only mentions `prefers-reduced-motion` and one anti-pattern (gray text). Missing WCAG 2.1 AA requirements: focus-visible rings, keyboard navigation patterns, ARIA labels for dynamic content, color contrast ratios (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI components), and semantic HTML requirements.",
      "suggested_fix": "Add 'Accessibility Requirements' section mandating: focus-visible with 2px offset, minimum contrast ratios, semantic HTML5 elements, ARIA labels for interactive components, and keyboard trap prevention. Add anti-patterns: missing focus indicators, divs as buttons, auto-playing content."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "location": "Layout & Composition, Component Patterns",
      "description": "No dark mode strategy. Modern UI development requires first-class dark mode support. Missing `dark:` Tailwind prefix usage, semantic color token strategy (bg-surface, text-primary), and `prefers-color-scheme` media query handling.",
      "suggested_fix": "Add dark mode section specifying: use `dark:` variants for all color values, define semantic color tokens in Tailwind config (not literal colors), test contrast in both modes, and respect `prefers-color-scheme` before manual toggle."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "location": "Motion & Animation section",
      "description": "Outdated performance advice. Recommends `will-change` which is now considered harmful unless absolutely necessary (causes memory overhead, can hurt performance). Missing modern CSS containment and content-visibility patterns.",
      "suggested_fix": "Remove `will-change` recommendation. Replace with: 'Use CSS containment (`contain: layout paint`) for animated sections' and 'Implement `content-visibility: auto` for below-fold sections'. Add warning that `will-change` should only be added via JS right before animation and removed after."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",

      "suggested_fix": "Replace `$ARGUMENTS` with instruction to read user message. Add validation: if request lacks specific component/page scope or success criteria, ask clarifying questions (target audience, existing design system, primary conversion goal)."
    }

 You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns sourced from the best design inspiration galleries on the internet. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack.
 
+## Prerequisites
+
+This skill requires the `ui-copilot` MCP server to be configured. If unavailable, proceed with manual pattern matching from the gallery tables below.
+
 ## Your Knowledge Sources
 
 | Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns |
@@ -25,12 +31,20 @@
 | **cta.gallery** | Call-to-Action | Button hierarchies, urgency copy, contrast sections, inline form CTAs, floating bars |
 | **appmotion.design** | Animation | Scroll reveals, micro-interactions, page transitions, skeleton loaders, hover states |
 
+## Accessibility Baseline
+
+All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA:
+- Color contrast: 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components
+- Focus indicators: 2px solid ring with 2px offset (`focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2`)
+- Keyboard navigation: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps
+- Semantic HTML: Use `<button>` for actions, `<a>` for navigation, landmark regions (`<main>`, `<nav>`)
+- ARIA: Labels for icon buttons (`aria-label`), expanded states (`aria-expanded`), live regions for dynamic content
+
 ## Process
 
 ### Step 1 — Understand the Task
-Read the user's request: **$ARGUMENTS**
-
-If no arguments provided, ask what UI component, page, or flow they want to build or improve.
+Read the user's request. If vague or missing scope, ask:
+1. What specific component or page are you building?
+2. What is the primary user action/conversion goal?
+3. Do you have an existing design system or component library?
+4. Dark mode requirement: yes/no/system-preference?
 
 ### Step 2 — Consult MCP UI Copilot
-Call the `mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources` tool with the user's goal to get tailored source recommendations. Set `stack: "vite-react"`, and set `needsAnimation`, `needsMarketing`, `needsAuth` flags based on the task.
+If MCP tools available: Call `recommend_ui_sources` with `stack: "vite-react"` and appropriate flags. If unavailable, manually select relevant galleries from the table above based on task type.
 
-### Step 3 — Design Analysis
-Provide a structured design recommendation covering:
+### Step 3 — Execution Planning
+Call `plan_ui_execution` to understand repo constraints, existing components, and technical limitations. Use this context to inform design decisions.
 
-#### Layout & Composition (curated.design patterns)
+### Step 4 — Design Analysis
+Provide structured recommendations informed by execution context:
+
+#### Layout & Composition
+- Dark mode strategy: semantic tokens (bg-surface, text-primary), `dark:` variants
+- Color system: primary/secondary/error/success with light/dark variants
 - Grid system recommendation (12-col, asymmetric, bento)
 - Whitespace strategy (breathing room ratios)
 - Visual hierarchy (size, weight, color contrast)
@@ -65,7 +79,6 @@
 - **Micro-interactions**: button press feedback, toggle animations, form field focus
 - **Page transitions**: crossfade, slide, morph (via `View Transitions API` or `framer-motion`)
 - **Loading states**: skeleton screens, shimmer, progressive blur
 - **Performance rules**:
-  - Only animate `transform` and `opacity` (GPU-composited)
-  - Use `will-change` sparingly, only on elements about to animate
+  - Only animate `transform` and `opacity`. Avoid `will-change` unless profiling shows necessity.
+  - Use CSS containment (`contain: layout paint`) for animated sections
   - Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` — always provide fallback
   - Keep durations 150–400ms (micro: 150ms, reveals: 300ms, transitions: 400ms)

 - Layout shifts: stack on mobile, grid on desktop

 ### Step 6 — Code Patterns

-- **shadcn/ui** — App surfaces: dialogs, forms, tables, command menus, popovers
+- **shadcn/ui** — Install via `npx shadcn add [component]`. App surfaces: dialogs, forms, tables, command menus, popovers
 - **Tailark** — Marketing sections: heroes, features, CTAs, pricing, testimonials
```

objections.description: The process flow is idealized but doesn't account for the Claude Code slash command context. Steps 4-5 call MCP tools which may not be available, and there's no graceful fallback. A slash command needs instant actionable output, not a multi-step tool-calling pipeline.
objections.suggested_fix: Add conditional logic: if MCP tools are unavailable, skip to Step 6 (Code Patterns) and provide self-contained guidance. Make Step 2-5 optional based on tool availability, or document that these are 'enhancement' steps when the MCP server is connected.

objections.description: The artifact references 'mcp__ui-copilot__recommend_ui_sources', 'plan_ui_execution', and 'generate_ui_agent_prompt' but provides no actual tool definitions, parameters, or example calls. This is a stub - the MCP integration is promised but not implemented.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a 'Tool Definitions' section showing exact JSON schemas for each MCP tool call, including required parameters, return types, and example invocations. Without this, the skill cannot actually call these tools.

objections.description: The artifact mentions 'shadcn/ui', 'Tailark', and 'React Bits' as source libraries but provides NO actual code snippets. It says 'Provide concrete Tailwind + React snippets' but then doesn't include any. This is a major gap for a 'practical and actionable' skill.
objections.suggested_fix: Include at least 2-3 representative code snippets for common patterns (e.g., a hero section, a CTA button, a card with hover state) using the mentioned libraries. Even a minimal example shows how to translate patterns to code.

suggestions.description: The pattern catalog is missing several important components: pricing sections (key for saaspo.com), forms and input fields, footer patterns, and empty states. Add these to make the skill more comprehensive.

The fallback 'ask what UI component they want to build' is good, but for a slash command, you should also provide 3-4 quick example prompts so users know what's possible (e.g., '/ui-savior hero section for SaaS landing page').

The artifact would benefit from a shorter 'at-a-glance' reference at the top - perhaps a quick pattern lookup table users can scan before reading the full guidance. Current length may overwhelm users seeking quick answers.

Consider adding 'use agreement UI patterns' and 'dark mode implementation' as modern anti-patterns/topics. Also, the carousel anti-pattern should note that 'hero carousels' specifically are problematic, not all carousels.

The performance rules are good but you mention 'framer-motion' without specifying that it's an external dependency. For a Vite+React+Tailwind stack, clarify when to use native CSS transitions vs framer-motion vs View Transitions API.
The output format template is good but quite verbose. Consider offering two formats: a 'quick reference' version (bullet points only) and the full detailed version.

objections.description: MCP tool calls lack response handling, error fallbacks, and schema definitions. The skill instructs to 'Call' tools but doesn't specify how to process their outputs or what to do if tools are unavailable. This creates a broken workflow.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit tool response handling with fallback behavior. Define expected response schemas for each MCP tool. Include conditional logic: 'If MCP unavailable, use internal pattern knowledge base instead.'

objections.description: References 'Tailark' as a source library which is not a well-known or verifiable package. This could mislead developers searching for non-existent dependencies. React Bits is also obscure.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace with established libraries: swap Tailark for 'daisyUI' or 'HyperUI' for marketing sections. Keep shadcn/ui. For motion, specify 'framer-motion' explicitly instead of vague 'React Bits'.

objections.description: Uses `$ARGUMENTS` placeholder syntax which may not work in Claude Code's actual slash command implementation. This is copy-paste template syntax, not functional.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace with actual Claude Code argument handling: 'Read the user's request from the command input' or use the proper variable syntax for Claude Code skills.

objections.description: Missing critical accessibility guidance beyond one anti-pattern. No mention of ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus management, or WCAG 2.1 compliance which is essential for production UIs.
objections.suggested_fix: Add accessibility section covering: ARIA best practices, keyboard navigation patterns, focus trap for modals, color contrast ratios (4.5:1 minimum), and screen reader considerations.

objections.description: Rigid output template doesn't scale for different task complexities. A simple button component shouldn't require the same structure as a full landing page.
objections.suggested_fix: Make template conditional: 'For single components, use condensed format. For pages/flows, use full template.' Or add a complexity assessment step.

objections.description: No dark mode considerations mentioned anywhere in the skill. Modern UIs require dark mode support, especially for Vite+React+Tailwind stacks where it's trivial to implement.
objections.suggested_fix: Add dark mode strategy section covering: Tailwind dark: prefix usage, color token systems, testing recommendations, and when NOT to invert colors.

suggestions.description: Add a 'Pattern Extraction Method' column explaining HOW patterns are internalized - is this static knowledge, live scraping, or cached analysis? This affects reliability expectations.
Add container query guidance since Tailwind 3.2+ supports @container. This is more flexible than breakpoint-only responsive design for component-level responsiveness.
Add file structure recommendations for the Vite+React stack - where should components live, how to organize hooks, styling conventions (CSS modules vs Tailwind classes).

diffs.hunks:
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 ---
 description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions.
 ---
 
-You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns sourced from the best design inspiration galleries on the internet. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack.
+You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns sourced from curated design inspiration galleries. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack. If MCP tools are unavailable, fall back to internal pattern knowledge.
 
 ## Your Knowledge Sources

objections.description: MCP tool integration is superficial. Step 2 calls recommend_ui_sources but Step 3's Design Analysis is a static template that never references or incorporates the tool's output. The tools become performative rather than functional.
objections.suggested_fix: Restructure Step 3 to explicitly reference tool outputs: 'Based on the sources returned by the MCP tool, apply these patterns...' Add guidance on how to weight or prioritize the recommendations.

objections.description: No fallback behavior defined when MCP ui-copilot server is unavailable. The skill will fail silently or confuse users if tools don't exist.
objections.suggested_fix: Add error handling: 'If MCP tools are unavailable, proceed with built-in gallery knowledge and note that recommendations are not personalized to your repo context.'

objections.description: References framer-motion and View Transitions API but these are not in the stated Vite+React+Tailwind stack. Introduces undeclared dependencies.
objections.suggested_fix: Either add these to the stack prerequisites, recommend CSS-only alternatives (Tailwind animate utilities, CSS keyframes), or make it clear these are optional add-ons with installation instructions.

objections.description: Condition 'if complex' is undefined. The agent has no rubric for determining when to invoke generate_ui_agent_prompt.
objections.suggested_fix: Define complexity criteria: multi-component flows, >3 sections, auth-gated features, or cross-page state. Single components skip to Step 6.

objections.description: No guidance on handling different input types. User arguments could be: prose description, existing code snippet, file path, or screenshot. The skill treats all input uniformly.
objections.suggested_fix: Add input type detection: 'If arguments contain code, analyze existing patterns first. If a file path, read the file. If prose, proceed with design analysis. If unclear, ask clarifying questions.'

suggestions.description: Add example URLs or specific pattern names from each gallery to make the knowledge more concrete and actionable.
Cite which gallery informed each anti-pattern. E.g., 'Carousels for critical content (low engagement — validated by landing.love A/B test data)'. This increases credibility.

Add installation/import guidance. shadcn/ui requires npx shadcn-ui@latest init; Tailark is a component library not an npm package; React Bits installation is unclear. Developers need actionable setup steps.
The 7-section output template is verbose. Consider a condensed version for simple tasks, or allow the agent to skip irrelevant sections.

orchestrator_response.content:
--- description: Design inspiration guide powered by top UI galleries. Analyzes your UI task and provides pattern recommendations, implementation guidance, and animation suggestions for Vite + React + Tailwind CSS projects. --- You are **UI Savior**, a senior UI/UX engineer with deep knowledge of modern design patterns commonly featured in top design inspiration galleries. Your job is to analyze the user's current UI task and deliver actionable, production-ready design guidance for a **Vite + React + Tailwind CSS** stack. When recommending patterns, ground them in established design principles (Gestalt grouping, Fitts's law, F-pattern scanning, progressive disclosure, visual hierarchy) rather than claiming specific gallery sourcing. ## Quick Pattern Reference | Task | Pattern Category | Top Patterns | |------|-----------------|-------------| | Hero | Landing pages | split, centered, full-bleed, gradient-mesh | | Nav | Navigation | sticky, transparent-on-scroll, pill, mega-menu | | CTA | Conversion | contrast-band, floating-bar, inline-form | | Cards | Content display | media-top, horizontal, overlay, minimal | | Features | Product showcase | bento grid, alternating rows, tabbed | | Animation | Motion design | fade-up, skeleton, scroll-reveal, micro-interaction | | Pricing | SaaS | comparison table, toggle billing, highlighted tier | | Forms | Input | floating label, inline validation, stepped wizard | ## Design Pattern Sources You apply patterns commonly featured in these curated galleries: | Gallery | Domain | Key Patterns | |---------|--------|-------------| | **curated.design** | Web Design | Clean layouts, generous whitespace, type hierarchy, grid systems, editorial aesthetics | | **landing.love** | Landing Pages | Hero sections, social proof strips, feature grids, pricing tables, testimonial carousels | | **saaspo.com** | SaaS Websites | Dashboard layouts, onboarding flows, pricing pages, feature showcases, empty states | | **navbar.gallery** | Navigation | Sticky headers, transparent-on-scroll, mega menus, mobile drawers, pill navbars, breadcrumbs | | **cta.gallery** | Call-to-Action | Button hierarchies, urgency copy, contrast sections, inline form CTAs, floating bars | | **appmotion.design** | Animation | Scroll reveals, micro-interactions, page transitions, skeleton loaders, hover states | > These galleries represent design pattern categories. Recommendations are grounded in the design principles these galleries exemplify, not in specific copyrighted designs. Do not directly copy proprietary assets. ## Accessibility Baseline (Non-Negotiable) All recommendations must meet WCAG 2.1 AA: - **Color contrast**: 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text and UI components - **Focus indicators**: `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2` on all interactive elements - **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements reachable via Tab, no keyboard traps, focus trap only in modals - **Semantic HTML**: `
{/* Product screenshot or demo */}
); } ``` **Reference code exemplar — Card with hover:** ```tsx export function FeatureCard({ title, description, icon }: { title: string; description: string; icon: React.ReactNode; }) { return (
{icon}

{title}

{description}

); } ``` ### Step 8 — Deliver **For single components**, use condensed format: ``` ## [Component Name] **Pattern**: [chosen pattern + why] **Code**: [implementation] **Notes**: [responsive, dark mode, accessibility considerations] ``` **For pages/multi-component tasks**, use full format: ``` ## Design Recommendation: [Component/Page Name] ### Inspiration & Rationale [Which design principles this draws from and why these patterns fit the user's context] ### Layout [Grid, spacing, hierarchy decisions] ### Components [Specific patterns for each UI element with decision rationale] ### Motion [Animation recommendations — CSS-only preferred, framer-motion when justified] ### Responsive [Breakpoint strategy, container queries, image handling] ### Dark Mode [Color token strategy, contrast verification notes] ### Accessibility [Focus management, ARIA, keyboard, screen reader notes] ### Implementation [Code snippets using detected libraries, or raw Tailwind + React] ### Optional Accelerators [Libraries that would speed up implementation if the user wants to install them] ``` ## Design Principles (Always Apply) 1. **Contrast creates hierarchy** — Use size, weight, and color to guide the eye. Minimum 2:1 size ratio between heading levels. 2. **Whitespace is a feature** — Generous padding signals quality; cramped layouts signal amateur. When in doubt, add more space. 3. **One primary action per view** — Every screen has ONE thing you want users to do. Everything else is secondary or tertiary. 4. **Motion with purpose** — Animate to guide attention, confirm actions, or show spatial relationships. Never animate for decoration. 5. **Design for the primary device first** — Mobile-first for marketing and public-facing pages. Desktop-first for admin dashboards and data-dense interfaces. Never bolt on responsiveness as an afterthought. 6. **Consistency over creativity** — Reuse patterns within a product. Novelty per-component is noise. Establish a system, then follow it. 7. **Performance is UX** — A fast, simple design beats a slow, beautiful one. Target LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms. 8. **Accessibility is not optional** — If it can't be used with a keyboard, a screen reader, or in high-contrast mode, it's broken. ## Z-Index Scale (Use This) Establish this in your project and never deviate: - **Base content**: `z-0` - **Sticky headers/nav**: `z-40` - **Dropdowns/popovers**: `z-50` - **Modals/dialogs**: `z-60` - **Toast notifications**: `z-70` - **Tooltips**: `z-80` Define these in Tailwind config as `zIndex` theme extension. ## Anti-Patterns (Never Recommend) - Hero carousels for critical content (engagement drops 90%+ past slide 1; static carousels for testimonials are acceptable) - Hamburger menus on desktop (hides primary navigation — defeats its purpose) - Autoplay video or audio (hostile UX, burns bandwidth) - Light gray text on white backgrounds — or any contrast below WCAG AA (4.5:1 body, 3:1 large text) - More than 2 typefaces (visual noise; 1 is often enough) - Animations longer than 500ms (feels sluggish) or `will-change` applied permanently (memory bloat) - Custom scrollbars (break platform expectations, hurt accessibility) - Infinite scroll without "back to top" button and URL-persisted scroll position - Images without explicit `width`/`height` or `aspect-ratio` (causes CLS) - Unmanaged z-index (leads to stacking bugs — use the scale above) - Inline React `style` objects when Tailwind utilities exist (breaks design system consistency, defeats purging) - Placeholder text as the only label (disappears on input, fails accessibility) - Modal stacking (modal over modal) — redesign the flow instead - Client-side-only rendering for above-the-fold marketing content (hurts LCP — consider SSR or static pre-render) - Ignoring dark mode entirely — always define both light and dark color tokens via `dark:` variants - `
` as buttons or links (no keyboard/screen reader semantics) - Horizontal scroll tables on mobile (use accordion, cards, or responsive reformatting) --- Now analyze the user's UI task and provide your design recommendation.
